jokes.text: "Wet cement." Is there any sweeter sign? Well, maybe "high voltage."
jokes.punchline: High voltage being even sweeter - revealing dangerous impulses
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - seemingly innocent observation reveals his attraction to danger/mischief. Good structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Hello, son. You want to try this flying motorcycle I just invented? - No time. - Okay.
jokes.setup: Homer casually offers incredibly dangerous invention to child
jokes.punchline: Both treat it as completely normal, Bart just too busy
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Homer's oblivious parenting and casual acceptance of rejection.

jokes.text: Like Stonehenge, this site will forever be a mystery. Who was Bart? And how did he manage to write his name in solid cement?
jokes.setup: Future civilization treating Bart's graffiti as mysterious artifact
jokes.punchline: Comparing simple vandalism to ancient mysteries
jokes.explanation: Clever premise of future misunderstanding. Good absurdist escalation.

jokes.text: He must've been smarter than his sister Lisa, about whom we know nothing.
jokes.setup: Future civilization analyzing Bart vs Lisa
jokes.punchline: Ironically concluding Bart was smarter when Lisa is the genius
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic reversal playing on established character dynamics.

jokes.text: Ay caramba!
jokes.punchline: Still using his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character consistency gag.

jokes.text: What's normal to him amazes us. - He will be our new god.
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to worship
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but fairly generic sci-fi parody.

jokes.text: Good, somebody's shadow. I'll just turn and brag about my work.
jokes.punchline: Explicitly stating his intention to show off
jokes.explanation: Bart's self-awareness about his showing off is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What would Jebediah Springfield say? - He'd be cool with it.
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal of historical figure
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart deflection with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Bart, you've graffito-tagged public property. It was an accident.
jokes.setup: Marge using fancy terminology for vandalism
jokes.punchline: Bart's absurd claim that writing his name in cement was accidental
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's pretentious language vs Bart's ridiculous excuse

jokes.text: What for? Nobody cared when Bogart defaced that sidewalk in Hollywood.
jokes.punchline: Comparing Bart to Hollywood legend's handprints
jokes.explanation: Homer's misunderstanding of Chinese Theatre ceremony is mildly clever.

jokes.text: It's been going down since the lake caught fire.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of environmental disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - treating ecological disaster as minor inconvenience with great economy.

jokes.text: Mom, when you give that lecture, you're boring Springfield.
jokes.setup: Marge's speech about being part of Springfield
jokes.punchline: Bart turns her metaphor against her
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's perfectly timed and very Bart-like in its clever rudeness.

jokes.text: Sorry to repeat myself, but it'll help you remember.
jokes.setup: Marge repeating her town pride speech
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the repetition as intentional technique
jokes.explanation: Marge's earnest acknowledgment of her teaching technique is endearing.

jokes.text: Wow, that does work.
jokes.punchline: Bart genuinely impressed by the method
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing the technique actually working on Bart.

jokes.text: When it comes to catching trout, nothing beats the German light infantry.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre military comparison for fishing technique
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Homer observation that makes no sense but sounds authoritative.

jokes.text: Beautiful, aren't they? - Yep. I'm gonna huck them at cars.
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate plan to use them as projectiles
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Homer's aesthetic appreciation vs Bart's destructive impulses.

jokes.text: It's clearly booming, Bart.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response to obviously poor sales
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm but fits Milhouse's defeated personality.

jokes.text: I don't even want any. I just bought a pity glass.
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's charity, not desire for product
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's underlying friendship despite his meanness.

jokes.text: We've squozen our whole supply.
jokes.punchline: Made-up past tense of 'squeeze'
jokes.explanation: Creative malapropism that sounds plausible and fits child speech patterns.

jokes.text: Thar she grows.
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'thar she blows' whaling cry
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Good friends, lots of lemons, numerous angel sightings.
jokes.setup: Bart listing good things about Springfield
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of supernatural events
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to supernatural presented matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Springfield sucks.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction from rival town kid
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy, though simple setup. The contrast creates the humor.

jokes.text: Why don't you make me. - I don't make trash, I burn it.
jokes.punchline: Bart's clever literal interpretation and insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback with clever wordplay and perfect Bart attitude. Great economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Then I guess you're a garbage man. - I know you are, but what am I?
jokes.setup: Logical response to Bart's burn comment
jokes.punchline: Bart falls back on childish retort
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's clever comeback backfiring and reverting to kid logic.

jokes.text: Takes one to know one. - Checkmate.
jokes.punchline: Declaring victory with chess terminology
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist ending to childish argument with unexpected elevated terminology.

jokes.text: Stop wearing your backpack over one shoulder. We invented that.
jokes.punchline: Claiming ownership of mundane fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation to ridiculous territorial claims over trivial things.

jokes.text: I'll kick your butt... at Nintendo.
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's video game challenge
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates tension appropriately for children's show.

jokes.text: I just put a rock in your crummy town. - That's a crud rock. It belongs in Crudtown.
jokes.setup: Petty act of placing rock across town line
jokes.punchline: Bart's improvised insult town name
jokes.explanation: Decent improvised insult that shows how petty the rivalry has become.

jokes.text: Look at the weak little baby. You're stupid, you stupid, weak baby.
jokes.punchline: Redundant, uncreative insult pattern
jokes.explanation: Intentionally weak insult showing the kid's lack of creativity, but not particularly funny itself.

jokes.text: Hey, everybody, an old man's talking.
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart dismissiveness with good economy, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: ...govern justly and grow vast fields of hemp for making rope and blankets.
jokes.punchline: Innocent hemp reference that sounds suspicious to modern ears
jokes.explanation: Clever historical joke playing on hemp's legitimate vs. recreational uses.

jokes.text: Yes, and marry our cousins. - I was... What are you talking about, Shelbyville?
jokes.punchline: Revealing disturbing motivation for the journey
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy reveal that recontextualizes the whole founding story.

jokes.text: Because they're so attractive. I thought that was the whole point of this journey.
jokes.punchline: Casual, shallow reasoning for disturbing practice
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Shelbyville's warped priorities with casual delivery.

jokes.text: Well, then we'll form our own town. Who will come and live a life devoted to chastity, abstinence... and a flavorless mush I call rootmarm?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly unappealing lifestyle culminating in gross food
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from moral to absurd with great made-up word 'rootmarm'.

jokes.text: ...lemons being the sweetest fruit available at the time.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they planted a lemon tree for sweetness
jokes.punchline: Implies lemons were actually sweet option in that era
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist historical joke about pioneer-era food scarcity.

jokes.text: If you don't learn Roman numerals... you'll never know when movies were copyrighted.
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining importance of Roman numerals
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and useless application
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on pointless education with perfect specificity and truth.

jokes.text: Whatever. I tried.
jokes.setup: Students running off during important lesson
jokes.punchline: Teacher's immediate resignation to failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflated teacher response with excellent economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: No. I said there's no time to explain, and I stick by that.
jokes.setup: Logical suggestion to just explain quickly
jokes.punchline: Stubborn adherence to arbitrary rule
jokes.explanation: Excellent Milhouse moment showing his rigid thinking and misplaced pride. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, they're always eating candy in Shelbyville. They love the sweet taste.
jokes.punchline: Homer's redundant explanation of why people eat candy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obviousness but not particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: We'll get it back or choke their rivers with our dead.
jokes.setup: Bart rallying kids to get tree back
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent imagery from child
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation with epic war imagery from kids' dispute.

jokes.text: I choose to take that literally. - Death to Shelbyville!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to interpret Bart's words positively
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately proving her wrong with war cry
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast and timing showing Marge's optimism immediately undermined.

jokes.text: Yes, Bart's a tutor now. Tute on, son. - Tute on.
jokes.setup: Marge's misinterpretation that Bart is tutoring
jokes.punchline: Homer's support with made-up verb form
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay with Homer's supportive but clueless parenting.

jokes.text: Nelson's the tough guy. Martin's the smart guy. Todd's the religious guy who ends up going crazy.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific prediction about Todd's arc
jokes.explanation: Good adventure story trope parody with ominous foreshadowing about Todd.

jokes.text: If you get lost, remember... you can always find east by staring directly at the sun.
jokes.punchline: Dangerous and wrong navigation tip
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - sounds helpful but is completely wrong and dangerous. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Put a pinch of sage in your boots... and all day long, a spicy scent is your reward.
jokes.punchline: Completely useless tip about boot aromatics
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Homer wisdom that prioritizes comfort over survival.

jokes.text: The fire hydrants here are yellow. This place is starting to freak me out.
jokes.punchline: Minor color difference causing existential dread
jokes.explanation: Good culture shock humor with small details feeling alien.

jokes.text: Radical. - They're getting rich off us. And that kid with the backpack said "radical." I say "radical." That's my thing that I say.
jokes.punchline: Bart's possessive outrage over common word
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing petty territorial behavior escalating.

jokes.text: Bart's quit his tutoring job and joined a violence gang.
jokes.punchline: Describing children's adventure as criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good Homer mischaracterization with adult perspective on kids' play.

jokes.text: Good idea. Milhouse, you and me will be Omega Team. Todd, you and Data are Team Strike Force. Nelson, that leaves you and Martin. - Team Discovery Channel.
jokes.setup: Bart giving teams dramatic military names
jokes.punchline: Nelson's mundane cable TV reference
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of military drama with 90s cultural reference. Nelson's unexpected contribution.

jokes.text: Your wussiness better come in handy.
jokes.punchline: Threatening Martin about his weakness being useful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line - threatening even when trying to be strategically positive.

jokes.text: But if they show up in the morgue, he's gonna fax us.
jokes.setup: Wiggum reporting on missing children search
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning death notification method
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Wiggum's inappropriate casualness about child death.

jokes.text: Honey, I was born in Shelbyville. - And it tears me up inside.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic reaction to mild revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's tendency to overdramatize.

jokes.text: If anything turned him bad... it's that time you let him wear a bathing suit instead of underwear.
jokes.setup: Discussing what might have corrupted Bart
jokes.punchline: Blaming bizarre, innocent wardrobe choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic connecting unrelated innocent event to moral corruption.

jokes.text: Pile in, everybody. No time to wipe your feet. - Come on.
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on cleanliness protocols during emergency
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer misplaced priorities joke but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: This is Country Time Lemonade Mix. There's never been anything close to a lemon in it. I swear.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about artificial lemonade
jokes.explanation: Good product placement parody with honest advertising.

jokes.text: Hey. And no one manhandles the bosom chum of Nelson Muntz.
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate term for friendship
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nelson character development with flowery language contrasting his toughness.

jokes.text: Spring forth, burly protector, and save me.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, elaborate plea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin vocabulary showing his pretentious but sincere friendship.

jokes.text: I never hang out with him, normally.
jokes.punchline: Immediate embarrassed backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment balancing his tough image with genuine friendship.

jokes.text: Hark to the tale of Nelson And the boy he loved so dear / They remained the best of friends For years and years and years
jokes.punchline: Spontaneous musical number about their bond
jokes.explanation: Creative musical interlude commenting on the friendship dynamics.

jokes.text: I thought you said you could read lips. - I assumed I could.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he just guessed at the ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse logic - overconfident assumptions with honest admission of failure.

jokes.text: You leave that to the baron and me.
jokes.punchline: Referring to himself with noble title
jokes.explanation: Standard Martin pretentiousness but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I don't go to school. - Okay, what's two plus two? - Five. - Story checks out.
jokes.setup: Testing if Bart really doesn't go to school
jokes.punchline: Wrong answer proving the claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic joke with perfect structure and economy. Bart's deliberate wrong answer works.

jokes.text: Radical. - Quit copying me.
jokes.setup: Bart using his catchphrase in disguise
jokes.punchline: Getting called out for copying himself
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the irony of Bart being accused of copying his own word.

jokes.text: No, not in my mouth... is what the kid would say.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly staying in character while objecting
jokes.explanation: Clever way of showing Bart's discomfort while maintaining his cover.

jokes.text: Maybe that's why we beat them at football nearly half the time, huh?
jokes.punchline: Statistic that actually shows they lose most of the time
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-defeating boast that reveals weakness while trying to show strength.

jokes.text: Yes. And that wig makes him look a lot like one of the Beatles.
jokes.punchline: Random Beatles comparison
jokes.explanation: Weak cultural reference without much setup or payoff.

jokes.text: I'll use these spray cans as jetpacks and fly to safety. So long, losers.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous physics-defying escape plan
jokes.explanation: Good cartoon physics joke with confident delivery.

jokes.text: Hey, look. Someone's attractive cousin. - Where? Where? - I don't see anybody.
jokes.punchline: Shelbyville kids' eager response to cousin reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to founding story with perfect character-specific distraction.

jokes.text: Slow down, you sidewalk-surfing cube-gleamers.
jokes.setup: Old man yelling at skateboarding kids
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate insult for skateboarders
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully creative old-man insult with excellent made-up terminology.

jokes.text: "Wet cement."
jokes.punchline: Callback to opening about wet cement being sweet
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural callback that pays off the opening perfectly.

jokes.text: I could sure use that flying motorcycle now. - You had your chance.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact response about missed opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Homer's characteristically unhelpful response.

jokes.text: "All other rooms contain man-eating tigers."
jokes.setup: Bart looking for exit from building
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous warning about impossible room contents
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist sign gag that escalates the danger absurdly.

jokes.text: Roman numerals. They never even tried to teach us that in school.
jokes.setup: Bart needing to read Roman numerals
jokes.punchline: Blaming school while we know he never pays attention
jokes.explanation: Good callback with typical Bart blame-shifting when he literally just skipped that lesson.

jokes.text: Correction: The only thing that's over is that transmission.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse taking radio protocol literally
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse pedantry showing his literal-minded approach to communication.

jokes.text: So this is what it feels like when doves cry.
jokes.punchline: Prince song reference for melodramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference for Bart's dramatic moment, though fairly standard.

jokes.text: So Rocky V plus Rocky II equals Rocky VII: Adrian's Revenge!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to figure out Roman numerals
jokes.punchline: Wrong math leading to fictional sequel title
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture math with funny made-up sequel title.

jokes.text: This whole raid was as useless as that yellow, lemon-shaped rock over there. Wait a minute. There's a lemon behind that rock.
jokes.setup: Bart giving up, comparing failure to random object
jokes.punchline: Realization that the 'useless' comparison leads to discovery
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure where the throwaway comparison becomes the solution. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: The impenetrable fortress of suburbia.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic description of mundane facility
jokes.explanation: Good satirical description of bureaucratic obstacles as military targets.

jokes.text: You must stupider than you look. - Stupider like a fox!
jokes.punchline: Homer's malapropism of 'crazy like a fox'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism that accidentally makes the insult even better.

jokes.text: No one in history has ever done anything this clever.
jokes.punchline: Absurd overestimation of his scheme's brilliance
jokes.explanation: Good Bart ego moment, though the plan isn't that clever so it works as character comedy.

jokes.text: Faster, son! He's got a taste for meat now.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it like a wild predator
jokes.explanation: Good Homer escalation treating guard dog like wild animal.

jokes.text: Let's shut the gate and seal them in. - Gun it, Flanders. - It won't start. Something's draining the battery. - Sorry.
jokes.punchline: Ned apologetically admitting to leaving something on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Ned's polite admission of responsibility.

jokes.text: Eat my shorts, Shelbyville. - Eat my shorts. - Eat my shorts. - Yes, eat all of our shirts.
jokes.punchline: Ralph's clothing confusion
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph malapropism showing his characteristic confusion.

jokes.text: Shake harder, boy.
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's inappropriate but innocent comment
jokes.explanation: Mild double entendre that's innocent from Grandpa's perspective.

jokes.text: And though Flanders was stuck with the impound fee... he could easily afford it.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Ned bearing the financial cost
jokes.explanation: Good acknowledgment of who actually pays for the adventure with typical Ned treatment.

jokes.text: They had banished the awful lemon tree forever... because it was haunted. Now, let's all celebrate... with a cool glass of turnip juice.
jokes.setup: Alternate version of the story from Shelbyville
jokes.punchline: Absurd reframing and gross celebratory drink
jokes.explanation: Excellent perspective flip showing how each side rewrites history, culminating in disgusting alternative.

jokes.text: Now, let's all celebrate... with a cool glass of turnip juice.
jokes.punchline: Celebrating with disgusting vegetable juice instead of lemonade
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Shelbyville perspective - awful alternative to stolen lemonade source
